ddls:
CREATE TABLE "api_clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API client */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the client */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* Unique API key for client authentication */,
  "email" TEXT /* Contact email for the client */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the client */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the client */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the client is active */,
  "register_date" TEXT /* Date when the client registered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API endpoint */,
  "endpoint_url" TEXT /* URL of the API endpoint */,
  "endpoint_description" TEXT /* Description of the endpoint's functionality */,
  "methods_allowed" TEXT /* HTTP methods allowed for the endpoint */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the API endpoint */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the API endpoint */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client who owns the endpoint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_endpoints_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES api_clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource */,
  "resource_name" TEXT /* Name of the resource */,
  "resource_description" TEXT /* Description of the resource */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the resource */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags for the resource */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the resource was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the resource was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pagination_config" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pagination configuration */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client this configuration applies to */,
  "default_offset" INTEGER /* Default offset for the client */,
  "default_limit" INTEGER /* Default limit for the client */,
  "max_limit" INTEGER /* Maximum limit allowed for the client */,
  "default_sort_by" TEXT /* Default sorting field for paginated results */,
  "default_sort_order" TEXT /* Default sorting order for paginated results */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pagination_config_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES api_clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request log */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client who made the request */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint that was requested */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the request */,
  "response_status" INTEGER /* HTTP status code of the response */,
  "offset" INTEGER /* Offset used in the request */,
  "limit" INTEGER /* Limit used in the request */,
  "request_method" TEXT /* HTTP method used in the request */,
  "request_body" TEXT /* Request body (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_logs_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES api_clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_logs_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES api_endpoints ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pagination_links" (
  "link_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pagination link */,
  "request_log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request log this link is associated with */,
  "next_link" TEXT /* URL for the next page of results */,
  "previous_link" TEXT /* URL for the previous page of results */,
  "first_link" TEXT /* URL for the first page of results */,
  "last_link" TEXT /* URL for the last page of results */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("link_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pagination_links_request_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_log_id") REFERENCES request_logs ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client accessing the resource */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the resource was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the resource was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_access_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES api_clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_access_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "query_parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each query parameter */,
  "request_log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request log this parameter is associated with */,
  "param_name" TEXT /* Name of the query parameter */,
  "param_value" TEXT /* Value of the query parameter */,
  "param_type" TEXT /* Type of query parameter (e.g., filter, sort, search) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_query_parameters_request_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_log_id") REFERENCES request_logs ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log */,
  "request_log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request log this error is associated with */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Description of the error */,
  "error_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the error */,
  "error_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the error */,
  "error_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error occurred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_request_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_log_id") REFERENCES request_logs ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rate_limits" (
  "limit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rate limit configuration */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client this configuration applies to */,
  "limit_type" TEXT /* Type of rate limit (e.g., requests per minute) */,
  "limit_value" INTEGER /* Value of the rate limit */,
  "time_period" TEXT /* Time period for the rate limit */,
  "remaining_requests" INTEGER /* Number of remaining requests within the time period */,
  "reset_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rate limit will reset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("limit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rate_limits_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES api_clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "usage_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage metric */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client this metric applies to */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint the metric is for */,
  "requests_count" INTEGER /* Number of requests made */,
  "data_transfer" REAL /* Amount of data transferred in MB */,
  "average_response_time" REAL /* Average response time for the API endpoint */,
  "error_rate" REAL /* Error rate for the API endpoint */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date for the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_usage_metrics_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES api_clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_usage_metrics_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES api_endpoints ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client this notification is for */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., rate limit warning, error alert) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification */,
  "send_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, failed, read) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES api_clients ("client_id")
)
